from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    # Helper to clean quoted objects
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip('"').strip("'")
    # Helper to build clause without subject
    def build_clause(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
        if pred == "floorCount":
            return f"{verb} {obj} floors"
        if pred == "bedCount":
            return f"{verb} {obj} beds"
        if pred == "numberOfRooms":
            return f"{verb} {obj} rooms"
        if pred == "country":
            if any(k in obj for k in ["States", "Kingdom", "Republic", "Union", "Islands", "Federated"]):
                return f"{verb} the {obj}"
        return f"{verb} {obj}"

    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Separate location triples (if any) from the rest
        location_objs = [clean_obj(tr.object) for tr in group if tr.predicate == "location"]
        other_triples = [tr for tr in group if tr.predicate != "location"]

        # Build main clause parts for the primary subject
        main_parts = []
        handled = set()
        for tr in other_triples:
            # handle special cases that need full sentences
            if tr.predicate in {"buildingStartDate", "inaugurationDate"}:
                if tr.predicate == "buildingStartDate":
                    main_parts.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                else:
                    main_parts.append(f"The inauguration of {subject} was on {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "leader":
                subj = subject
                if any(k in subj for k in ["Republic", "Kingdom", "States", "Union", "Islands", "Federated"]):
                    subj = "the " + subj
                main_parts.append(f"The leader of {subj} is {clean_obj(tr.object)}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)
            elif tr.predicate == "height":
                obj = clean_obj(tr.object).replace(" (", " ").replace(")", "")
                main_parts.append(f"The height of {subject} is {obj}")
                handled.add(tr.predicate)

        # Preferred ordering for remaining predicates
        order = ["architect", "buildingType", "completionDate", "floorCount", "cost", "floorArea"]
        for pred in order:
            for tr in other_triples:
                if tr.predicate == pred and pred not in handled:
                    main_parts.append(build_clause(pred, clean_obj(tr.object)))
                    handled.add(pred)
                    break

        # Add any other predicates not yet handled
        for tr in other_triples:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            main_parts.append(build_clause(tr.predicate, clean_obj(tr.object)))

        # Start sentence with the subject (unless we only have location info)
        sentence = ""
        if location_objs:
            # Use first location as primary location phrase
            loc = location_objs[0]
            sentence = f"{subject} is located in {loc}"
            # If there are additional location objects, list them
            if len(location_objs) > 1:
                sentence += " and " + " and ".join(location_objs[1:])
        else:
            sentence = subject

        if main_parts:
            sentence += ", " + ", ".join(main_parts)

        # Attach information about the location entity if we have its own triples
        if location_objs:
            loc_entity = location_objs[0]
            if loc_entity in subject_groups:
                loc_triples = [t for t in subject_groups[loc_entity] if t.predicate != "location"]
                loc_clauses = []
                for lt in loc_triples:
                    # skip redundant location back‑reference
                    if lt.predicate == "country":
                        continue
                    loc_clauses.append(build_clause(lt.predicate, clean_obj(lt.object)))
                if loc_clauses:
                    sentence += f", which " + ", ".join(loc_clauses)

        sentence = sentence.rstrip() + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
